# Mundabra AI Guardrails

Lightweight TypeScript guardrails for the Vercel AI SDK and agentic applications.

## Summary

This package wraps AI SDK language models with input and output guards.

It supports:

- Prompt injection detection
- Encoding and obfuscation detection
- Input length limiting
- Topic filtering
- PII detection and redaction
- Secret detection
- Content safety heuristics
- Exfiltration detection
- Optional LLM classifier guards

## Public API

- `withGuardrails(model, config?)`
- `createGuardEngine(config)`
- `buildMiddleware(engine)`
- `defineGuard(...)`

## Defaults

`withGuardrails(model)` enables:

- Input: `injection`, `encoding`, `length`
- Output: `pii`, `secrets`, `exfiltration`

It does not enable:

- `content`
- `topic`
- LLM classifier

## Execution Model

- Guards run by stage: `input` or `output`
- Guards run by tier: `1`, `2`, `3`
- Guards execute sequentially within each tier so redactions compose
- Tier 2 and tier 3 default to `runOn: "flagged"`
- Tier 2 and tier 3 can opt into `runOn: "always"`

## Middleware Behavior

`transformParams`:

- extracts user text from AI SDK prompt messages
- runs input guards before the model call

`wrapGenerate`:

- extracts generated text content
- runs output guards
- rewrites generated text parts when a guard returns `redact`

`wrapStream`:

- buffers the entire model stream
- accumulates text deltas
- runs output guards after completion
- replays sanitized chunks to the caller

Important consequence:

- stream safety is prioritized over real-time token streaming

## Important Invariants

- ESM-only package
- Node.js `>=20`
- No extra runtime dependencies beyond peer deps
- Redaction behavior must be covered in tests for both generate and stream paths
- Stripe publishable keys such as `pk_live_...` and `pk_test_...` are public and must not be flagged as secrets
- README and machine-readable docs must stay aligned with real defaults and limitations

## Disclaimer

- The package is provided as a practical local guardrails layer, not as a complete AI safety or security platform.
- It must not be described as a guarantee of compliance, confidentiality, or safety.
- It should not be used as the only control in high-risk or regulated deployments.

## Repository Map

- `src/index.ts`: public exports
- `src/types.ts`: config and guard contracts
- `src/engine.ts`: guard execution engine
- `src/middleware.ts`: AI SDK middleware integration
- `src/guards/input/*`: input guards
- `src/guards/output/*`: output guards
- `src/guards/classifiers/llm.ts`: optional LLM judge
- `src/utils/*`: normalization and helpers
- `tests/middleware.test.ts`: middleware regression coverage
- `tests/engine.test.ts`: engine semantics
- `tests/guards/*.test.ts`: per-guard tests

## Commands

- install: `pnpm install`
- test: `pnpm test`
- typecheck: `pnpm typecheck`
- build: `pnpm build`

## Recommended Reading

- `README.md`
- `docs/agent-guide.md`
- `docs/guard-reference.md`
- `AGENTS.md`
